channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Marwa calls for girl-child protection, seeks traditional rulers’ support against drug abuse
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Emir of Zazzau expresses hope for positive change at book launch By Kingsley Omonobi Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA), Brig.-Gen. Mohamed Buba Marwa (retd.), has called for stronger protection of the girl-child, saying addressing poverty, abuse

item.title: Human trafficking: Makinde reaffirms commitment to reintegration of returnees
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Engineer Seyi Makinde, has reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to building a sustainable reintegration system for returned migrants and survivors of human trafficking, saying the state will continue to work with federal institutions, international partners, and other

item.title: SERAP sues INEC over ‘failure to probe alleged N800 billion FAAC diversion for campaign funding’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has filed a lawsuit against the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) “over the failure to investigate the allegations that governors of the All Progressives Congress (APC) diverted N800 billion for political and campaign purposes.”

item.title: FG approves ₦50,000 WAEC, NECO registration fee from 2027
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has approved an increase in the registration fees for the West African Examinations Council (WAEC) and the National Examinations Council (NECO) Senior Secondary School Certificate Examinations (SSCE), raising the cost to ₦50,000 per candidate from 2027. The post FG approves ₦5

item.title: INEC extends presidential, National Assembly candidates’ submission deadline
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has extended by three days the deadline for political parties to submit the names and personal particulars of their candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections. The electoral body announced on Sunday that political parties

item.title: 2027 election: APC completes upload of 471 candidates to INEC portal
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has completed the upload of its 471 candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) Candidate Nomination Portal. The party submitted the names of its presidential and vice-presidential ca

item.title: England, Argentina Set Up World Cup Showdown After Quarter-Final Wins
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The winner of that game will take on the victor of France's semi-final with Spain, who meet in Texas on Tuesday, in the final on July 19. The post England, Argentina Set Up World Cup Showdown After Quarter-Final Wins appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Oriire pupils’ rescue: Collaboration key to tackling insecurity, says Gani Adams

item.description: Commends FG, Oyo government, security agenciesCalls for greater investment in intelligence gathering By Dapo Akinrefon The Aare Ona Kakanfo of Yorubaland, Iba Gani Adams, has commended the collaborative efforts of the Federal Government, Oyo State Government, security agencies and local security gro

item.title: Yoruba Leaders Launch Fundraising Campaign To Combat Banditry, Kidnapping, Terrorism
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Yoruba leaders under the aegis of the Council of Yoruba Elders (CYE) have launched a fundraising campaign aimed at supporting security efforts to tackle banditry, kidnapping and terrorism across 10 states in Nigeria. The council said the initiative was designed to provide resources, including se

item.title: PFIPC fallout: Police visited my home in Ogbomoso twice, seized my phones — Adeyemi’s father
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The father of Prince Adeniyi Mathew Adeyemi, Pa Adeniyi Adetunji, has disclosed that operatives of the Nigeria Police Force have visited his Ogbomoso home twice in search of his son. The post PFIPC fallout: Police visited my home in Ogbomoso twice, seized my phones — Adeyemi’s father appeared first

item.title: ABA Concludes Referees, Technical Officials Clinic With Call To Shape Nigerian Basketball’s Future
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Amateur Basketball Academies (ABA) has successfully concluded the maiden edition of its Referees and Technical Officials Clinic in Abuja, with organisers describing the programme as a significant milestone in the development of basketball officiating in Nigeria. More than 25 young participants r

item.title: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

item.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham, Trump’s close ally, dies
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He had just returned from Kyiv, where he met the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.

item.title: Yoruba elders alert of plot by Fulani to conquer South-West, rally eminent Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Council of Yoruba Elders (CYE) has raised the alarm over an alleged plot by the Fulani to conquer South-Western Nigeria. The post Yoruba elders alert of plot by Fulani to conquer South-West, rally eminent Nigerians appeared first on Tribune Online.

Mohamed Marwa (Rtd), has called for the protection of the girl-child against systemic vulnerabilities that drive women and youth towards drug abuse and exploitation. The post Drug abuse: Marwa ca

item.title: Omisore is staying in APC, defections have nothing to do with him — Famurewa
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Hon. Ajibola Israel Famurewa is a two-term Member of the House of Representatives from Atakumosa East-West Federal Constituency. He is currently the Director-General of the Osun Rescue Mission Team (formerly the Iyiola Omisore Campaign Organisation). The post Omisore is staying in APC, defections ha

item.title: How my song ‘Fall’ changed my life — Davido
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Davido has revealed the life-changing impact of his 2017 hit song “Fall,” revealing that the record transformed his career and significantly changed his financial status. The post How my song ‘Fall’ changed my life — Davido appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness', his office says
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The influential politician has just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.

The appointment was announced in a statement by the Governor’

item.title: Jawondo alleges fraud in APC Kwara governorship primary
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A member of the Kwara State All Progressives Congress (APC) Elders' Caucus and former Attorney General and Commissioner for Justice in the state, Salman Jawondo (SAN), has alleged that the party's 2026 governorship primary was marred by fraud. The post Jawondo alleges fraud in APC Kwara governorship

item.title: Breaking: NGX meets FTSE Rusell, international investors in London over T+1 concerns
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: A delegation of the Nigerian Exchange Group (NGX) led by its Chairman, Umaru Kwairanga, has met with FTSE Russell, international institutional investors, custodians, and other key capital market stakeholders in London to address concerns over the market’s T+1 settlement. The post Breaking: NGX meets

item.title: Senegal sack manager Thiaw in wake of World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Senegal sack manager Pape Thiaw in the aftermath of the country's exit at the last-32 stage of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Orire pupils’ rescue reflects Tinubu’s commitment to security, says Sanwo-Olu
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, has described the rescue of abducted schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State as evidence of President Bola Tinubu’s commitment to tackling insecurity and protecting the lives of Nigerians. In a stat

item.title: 40 More Nigerians Evacuated From South Africa As Sixth Return Flight Lands
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Forty more Nigerians stranded in South Africa have returned home as the sixth batch of evacuees arrived at the Murtala Muhammed International Airport, Lagos.   The Nigerians in Diaspora Commission (NiDCOM) announced the development in a statement shared on its official X handle on Saturday, say

item.title: Kidnapping: Gov Adeleke calls for increased momentum against criminals
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Osun State Governor, Ademola Adeleke has called on security agencies, particularly the Osun State Command of the Nigeria Police Force, to sustain the ongoing momentum against criminal elements following the successful rescue of abducted pupils and teachers in Oyo State. The governor made the appeal

item.title: Ali Pate Plays The Long Game
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: In Bauchi, where the sitting governor is an opposition figure, the role of party leader presenting the APC candidate to the national leader of the party, President Bola Tinubu, was played by Minister of Health, Ali Pate. The minister, it seems, is positioning himself to be kingmaker ahead of the Bau

item.title: Market survey reveals fall in food prices
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Rice, for instance, fell from ₦75,000 per 50kg bag in the last quarter of 2025 to ₦60,000 in June 2026. This represents a ₦15,000 decrease and a 20% drop.

item.title: France vs Spain World Cup semifinal: Prediction, Mbappe-Yamal, venue, stats
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: European football titans France and Spain meet in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup 2026 final four matchup in Dallas.

item.title: Ukraine sets Russian refinery ablaze in fresh deadly drone attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A man was killed and three other people, including a child, were injured in Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia’s Volga region of Samara, regional Governor Vyacheslav Fedorishchev said on Sunday. Residential buildings and an industrial facility were damaged in the attacks, Fedorishchev said on Te

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nollywood actor, Oluwole Cole, professionally known as Baraka, has stated that actress Mosunmola Filani is rude and pompous. He made the claim in his latest interview with the African A-List podcast. When asked about his encounter with the actress, Baraka said “Mosun Filani was very rude, and

item.title: Chasing South American Drug Cartels
item.dc:creator: Leadership

There is very little data or a comprehensive study on the amount of d

item.title: JUST-IN: NiMet Raises Fresh Flood Alarm For Bauchi, Adamawa, Benue, 23 Other States
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet) has issued a fresh flash flood warning for 26 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), placing Bauchi, Jigawa, Taraba, Adamawa, Plateau, Benue, Nasarawa and Kogi among northern states facing heightened flood risk between July 11 and July 20, 2026. Ni

item.title: ‘Guilty man crying’ – Presidency tackles Peter Obi over airport row
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu’s Senior Special Assistant on Media and Publicity, Temitope Ajayi, has described the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi as a guilty man who cries the most. Ajayi challenged Obi to be honourable enough to recant the deliberate mischaracteri

item.title: Rapper Odumodublvck reveals only regret in making music
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian rapper, Tochukwu Ojogwu, popularly known as Odumodublvck, has revealed his only “regret” in choosing music. The rapper, who has repeatedly stated that he settled for music after his ambition of becoming a footballer failed, said the only thing he regrets about ending up as a mus

item.description: Osun State Police Command has said it is following due process before taking any action over allegations that wanted criminals are hiding inside the Osun State Government House. The command also insisted that legal procedures must be observed before the premises can be accessed. The command’s

item.description: England and Argentina have qualified for the next round ooooh....We have gotten to the stage where high blood pressure becomes permanent....Please dont watch thse matches if you are not in good health or if you have high BP..

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Iran confirmed the attacks, with local news IRNA quoting an Iranian official as saying US forces attacked five cities in the Bushehr province. The post US strikes Iran again, Iran attacks Bahrain, Qatar, Jordan, Kuwait appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: US senator and Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Prominent US Senator Lindsey Graham, a leading Republican and close ally of President Donald Trump, died on Saturday aged 71 after a "brief and sudden illness" his office said in a statement.

item.title: FG increases WAEC, NECO registration fees to N50,000
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The examinations are conducted for internal candidates who are finishing their Senior Secondary School education. WAEC just finished this year's exam a few weeks ago, while NECO's is ongoing. The post FG increases WAEC, NECO registration fees to N50,000 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Military confirms death of Army lieutenant in rescue of abducted Oyo pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Kingsley Omonobi Military sources have confirmed the death of an Army officer, Lieutenant F.A. Isaac, during the operation that rescued 44 abducted pupils and teachers from the Old Oyo National Park. The victims were rescued on July 10, 2026, in a coordinated operation involving security agencies

The Three Lions came from a goal down to win 2-1 in extra time at Hard Rock Stadium as Bellingham scored twice to set u

item.title: Navy destroys illegal refineries in Rivers, recovers 43,000 litres of petroleum products
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has dismantled four illegal refining sites in Rivers State and recovered about 43,000 litres of suspected illegally refined petroleum products, as it intensifies its campaign against crude oil theft and illegal refining activities in the Niger Delta. item.description: The strikes follow an attack on a Cyprus -flagged vessel travelling through the critical waterway.

item.title: Typhoon slams into eastern China after forcing nearly two million people to flee
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Typhoon Bavi weakened to a severe tropical storm on Sunday after making landfall in eastern China, where authorities evacuated nearly two million people and suspended transport as the powerful storm threatened flooding and destructive winds.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Senegal sack Pape Thiaw after disappointing campaign
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: The Senegal Football Federation has dismissed head coach Pape Thiaw following the country's underwhelming performance at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post World Cup 2026: Senegal sack Pape Thiaw after disappointing campaign appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Ghana slavery apology: Why many descendants say words are not enough
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Descendants of enslaved Africans say symbolic apologies cannot repair generations of loss and injustice.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Fixtures, date, time, venue of semi-finals revealed
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is in its most pivotal stage as the fixtures for the semi-finals have been confirmed after the conclusion of the quarter-final matches. The post 2026 World Cup: Fixtures, date, time, venue of semi-finals revealed appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Inter Milan move thrills Super Falcons star Toni Payne
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Nerazzurre confirmed the signing on Saturday, announcing that the Nigerian international has penned a two-year contract that will keep her at the club until June 2028 The post Inter Milan move thrills Super Falcons star Toni Payne appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, architect of modern Qatar
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Father Emir oversaw the meteoric rise of the small gas-rich nation.

item.title: Inside Ruto’s inner circle: Fixers, soldiers and gatekeepers shaping the 2027 race
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: With just over a year until the next general election, Kenya President William Ruto faces a challenging political landscape as pressure mounts from a unified opposition coalition aiming to unseat him. To secure a second term, Ruto will need maximum support from key allies within the ruling Kenya Kwa

item.title: Sunday Spontaneous Post
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: #SundayBlessings #GratefulHeart #FaithAndPeace #PositiveMindset #GentleSundayGood Morning....Sunday brings renewal.God will wash away the heaviness of the week and fill you with fresh light. You are not defined by the hate you receive , you are defined by the grace you carry.“This is the day the Lor

item.title: 2027 polls: INEC extends nomination deadline after parties’ appeal
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has extended the deadline for political parties to submit the names and personal particulars of their candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections following an appeal by political parties. The post 2027 polls: INEC extends

item.title: INEC extends deadline for parties to submit candidates for 2027 polls
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has extended the deadline for political parties to submit the names of their candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections by three days. The commission announced that the deadline, which was initially set to expire on Satur

item.title: Diamond couple: After fifty years of marriage, their marital love still intact
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Apostle Israel Oku and his wife celebrate 50 years of marriage, sharing their journey, enduring values and advice for young couples. The post Diamond couple: After fifty years of marriage, their marital love still intact appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: 2027: Pushing For Judicial Accountability And Electoral Justice
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: In this piece, ADEOLA AKINBOBOLA writes about the need for the judiciary to uphold integrity and sustain public trust as the race for the 2027 elections gathers momentum. As the 2027 elections approach, concerns about judicial integrity, electoral justice, and public trust in democratic institutions

item.title: World Cup 2026: List of top scorers as Bellingham joins race
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: The race for the top scorer award at the 2026 FIFA World Cup has intensified following the completion of the quarter-finals, with Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe leading the scoring charts. item.title: APC completes upload of 471 candidates to INEC portal

item.description: “With this milestone achieved, our focus now shifts to mobilisation and preparations for a successful campaign and decisive victory at the polls.”
